revision.text:
[[ja:平柳星宮神社]] {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Hirayanagi Hoshimiya Shrine (平柳星宮神社) | image = File:平柳星宮神社拝殿.jpg | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}} {{ill|Tochigi (city)|en|Tochigi (city)|qid=Q44830|lt=Tochigi City|1=Tochigi (city)}} {{ill|Hirayanagi Town|ja|平柳町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirayanagi Town|1=hirayanagichou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q56353178}} 1-23-26 | Position = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Iwanarekami|ja|磐裂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanarekami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Nerarekami|ja|根裂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerarekami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Keitsushinomi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keitsushinomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神|1=Futsunushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1107286}} | Shrine Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Founded = {{ill|Eikyō|en|Eikyō|qid=Q1193059|lt=Eikyō|1=Eikyō}} Year 2 ({{ill|1432|en|1432|qid=Q6349|lt=1432|1=1432}}) | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = Hirayanagi Daimyōjin / Koku-zō-san (平柳大明神・こくぞうさん) | Annual Festival = | Shinto Rituals = | Latitude = 36°23'28.8" | Longitude = 139°44'22.9" | Map = Japan Tochigi | location = {{ill|Tochigi|qid=Q44830|1=Tochigi (city)}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.391333|N|139.739694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Hirayanagi Hoshimiya Shrine''' (ひらやなぎほしのみやじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located at 1-23-26, Hirayanagi Town, Tochigi City, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. It serves as the guardian shrine of the former Hirayanagi Village. Although its formal name is simply "Hoshimiya Shrine" (星宮神社), it is commonly distinguished by its place name because there are several Hoshimiya Shrines within Tochigi Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank under the modern shrine Ranking system was that of a Village Shrine. During the Edo period the shrine was known as "Hirayanagi Daimyōjin" (平柳大明神), and at that time the principal image was {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|en|Ākāśagarbha|qid=Q416174|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|1=Ākāśagarbha}}, which is why it is still colloquially called "Koku-zō-san." It is also renowned as the "Shrine of Eels" Yomiuri Shimbun Tochigi edition, July 29, 2021, page 27. At this shrine the eel is regarded as a divine messenger, its determined progress through mud believed to bestow the power to overcome difficulties . == Enshrined Deities == {{ill|Iwanarekami|ja|磐裂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanarekami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ・ {{ill|Nerarekami|ja|根裂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerarekami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ・ {{ill|Keitsushinomi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keitsushinomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神|1=Futsunushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1107286}} == History == Originally, the shrine was located in what is now Ōmachi in Tochigi City, but it was relocated to its present site in the 2nd year of {{ill|Eikyō|en|Eikyō|qid=Q1193059|lt=Eikyō|1=Eikyō}} ({{ill|1432|en|1432|qid=Q6349|lt=1432|1=1432}}). == Shrine Grounds == [[File:平柳星宮神社なでうなぎ.jpg|thumb|Caressed Eel]] * Main Hall * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Shrine Office * Mikoshi Storehouse * Chōzuya (Purification Pavilion) * Nade Unagi * Kagura Hall * Tengu Hibā == Sub-Shrines on the Shrine Grounds == [[File:平柳星宮神社境内社白山神社相殿五社.jpg|thumb|Hakusan Shrine (the two on the left) and the Aikado Five Shrines]] * Hakusan Shrine (two shrines) * Aikado Five Shrines (Daikokusha, Inarisha, Shinmeisha, Suijinsha, and Yama-sha) [[File:平柳星宮神社境内社六社.jpg|thumb|The Six Sub-Shrines]] * Six Shrines (Suga Shrine, Ōsugi Shrine, Miyoya Shrine, Inari Shrine, Chikagata Shrine, and Santai Shrine) [[File:平柳星宮神社境内社猿田彦大神.jpg|thumb|Sarutahiko Ōkami]] * Sarutahiko Ōkami == Footnotes == {{reflist}} {{Japanese history stub}} {{Shinto stub}} {{Buddhism stub}} {{pref-stub-Tochigi Prefecture}} {{Shinto Side}} {{translated page|ja|平柳星宮神社|version=84756783|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:平柳星宮神社]] {{wikidata link|Q60989143}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tochigi, Tochigi]] [[Category:History of Tochigi, Tochigi]] [[Category:Hoshimiya Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Ākāśagarbha]]
